title: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
content: Central government income support scheme for landholding farmer families. Provides ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments of ₹2,000 directly into the bank account.
eligibility: All landholding farmer families subject to exclusion criteria (income tax payers in assessment year, constitutional post holders, etc.). Both small and marginal farmers are covered.
how_to_apply: Register through the PM-KISAN portal (pmkisan.gov.in) or visit the nearest agriculture department / Common Service Centre (CSC).

title: Ayushman Bharat – PM-JAY
content: World's largest health insurance scheme providing coverage up to ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization at empanelled public and private hospitals.
eligibility: Families identified as deprived based on SECC 2011 data for rural areas and defined occupational categories for urban areas. State-specific expansion may cover additional families.
documents: Ration card or SECC eligibility letter
how_to_apply: Check eligibility on pmjay.gov.in using mobile number or ration card. Visit an empanelled hospital or Ayushman Mitra for enrollment assistance.

title: Ration Card (PDS / NFSA)
content: State-issued card for subsidized food grains (rice, wheat, sugar) and other essentials through the Public Distribution System under the National Food Security Act.
eligibility: Indian residents meeting state-specific income and family criteria. Categories include APL, BPL, and Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) with different entitlements.
documents: Aadhaar cards of all family members
how_to_apply: Apply online through your state's food and civil supplies portal or visit the nearest ration office / CSC. Processing time varies by state.

title: Indian Passport Application
content: Official travel document issued by the Ministry of External Affairs through Passport Seva Kendras. Available as regular (36 pages) or jumbo (60 pages) booklet.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen. Minors require parental consent and additional documents.
documents: Proof of address (Aadhaar, utility bill, bank passbook)
Proof of date of birth (birth certificate, school leaving certificate, Aadhaar)
how_to_apply: Register on passportindia.gov.in, fill the application form, pay fees online, and book an appointment at the nearest Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) or Post Office PSK.

title: Aadhaar Enrollment and Update
content: 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI based on biometric and demographic data. Required for most government schemes and services.
eligibility: Any resident of India (including infants). Non-residents are not eligible.
documents: Proof of identity (PAN, passport, voter ID, etc.)
Proof of date of birth (for children under 5, only birth certificate needed)
how_to_apply: Visit an Aadhaar Enrollment Centre or book appointment on uidai.gov.in. Updates (address, mobile, photo) can be done online for supported fields.

title: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi NREGA)
content: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to every rural household whose adult members volunteer for unskilled manual work.
eligibility: Adult members of rural households willing to do unskilled manual work. One Job Card per household.
how_to_apply: Apply for a Job Card at the local Gram Panchayat office. Work is allocated through the Panchayat and wages are paid to bank/post office accounts.

title: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
content: Pension scheme for workers in the unorganized sector providing guaranteed minimum pension of ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 per month after age 60, depending on contribution.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18–40 years with a savings bank account. Must have Aadhaar and mobile number linked to the account.
how_to_apply: Enroll through your bank branch or net banking. Auto-debit of contributions from the linked bank account.

title: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
content: Central assistance of ₹200 per month (₹500 for persons above 80 years) plus state top-up for BPL persons aged 60 years and above.
eligibility: Persons aged 60+ belonging to BPL households as per state BPL list. State governments may have additional criteria.
documents: BPL ration card or BPL certificate
how_to_apply: Apply through the Block Development Office, Gram Panchayat, or state social welfare department portal.

title: PM Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
content: National mission for financial inclusion providing zero-balance bank accounts with RuPay debit card, accident insurance cover of ₹2 lakh, and overdraft facility up to ₹10,000.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen who does not have a bank account. One account per person.
documents: Aadhaar card (or other officially valid KYC document)
how_to_apply: Visit any bank branch or Business Correspondent (Bank Mitra) outlet with KYC documents. Account can be opened on the spot.

title: PM SVANidhi (Street Vendor Loan Scheme)
content: Special micro-credit facility providing collateral-free working capital loans up to ₹10,000 (first tranche), ₹20,000 (second), and ₹50,000 (third) to street vendors.
eligibility: Street vendors in urban areas with Certificate of Vending or Letter of Recommendation from Urban Local Body. Must have been vending before March 24, 2020.
documents: Vending certificate or ULB recommendation letter
how_to_apply: Apply through the PM SVANidhi portal (pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in) or through Urban Local Body / CSC.

title: National Scholarship Portal (NSP)
content: Single portal for applying to central and state government scholarships for school, college, and professional course students from SC, ST, OBC, minority, and economically weaker sections.
eligibility: Varies by scholarship scheme — typically based on category, family income, academic performance, and institution type. Check specific scheme on scholarships.gov.in.
how_to_apply: Register on scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal), select the relevant scheme, fill the application, and submit before the deadline.

title: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY)
content: Affordable housing scheme providing credit-linked subsidy on home loans for Economically Weaker Section (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG), and Middle Income Group (MIG) beneficiaries.
eligibility: Beneficiary family should not own a pucca house. Income limits vary: EWS up to ₹3 lakh, LIG ₹3–6 lakh, MIG-I ₹6–12 lakh, MIG-II ₹12–18 lakh per annum.
documents: Aadhaar cards of all family members
Property documents (for existing house) or land documents (for construction)
how_to_apply: Apply through pmaymis.gov.in or via participating banks and housing finance companies for credit-linked subsidy.

title: PM Ujjwala Yojana
content: Provides free LPG connections to women from BPL households to replace unclean cooking fuels. Includes deposit-free connection and first refill/flexible payment options.
eligibility: Adult women from BPL households without an existing LPG connection. SECC 2011 list or BPL ration card holders are prioritized.
documents: BPL ration card or SECC list reference
how_to_apply: Apply through your nearest LPG distributor or online via the PM Ujjwala portal. Distributor verifies eligibility and issues connection.

title: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
content: Crop insurance scheme providing financial support to farmers suffering crop loss or damage due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases. Low premium rates subsidized by government.
eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas — both loanee and non-loanee farmers can enroll.
how_to_apply: Enroll through banks (for loanee farmers), insurance companies, CSCs, or the PMFBY portal during the crop season enrollment window.

title: DigiLocker
content: Government cloud platform for issuing and verifying documents and certificates digitally. Linked documents are legally equivalent to physical originals under IT Act.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen with an Aadhaar number and mobile number.
how_to_apply: Sign up at digilocker.gov.in using Aadhaar OTP. Pull documents issued by registered issuers (CBSE, RTO, income tax, etc.) into your locker.
